airports.name: Dickinson – Theodore Roosevelt Regional Airport
airports.note: Closest airport to Medora. Limited daily flights (Denver connections). Best for a direct-to-the-Badlands arrival.

airports.name: Bismarck Airport
airports.note: More daily flights and connections than Dickinson. A scenic 1.5-hour drive west on I-94 (pass the Enchanted Highway).

airports.name: Williston Basin International Airport
airports.note: Northern approach. Pairs well with a North Unit / River Road trip.

airports.name: Rapid City Regional Airport
airports.note: Gateway to the Black Hills. Ideal if you want Mount Rushmore, Custer State Park and Devils Tower on the way up US-85 (the Theodore Roosevelt Expressway).

airports.name: Billings Logan International Airport
airports.note: Western gateway. The natural start for a Yellowstone + Beartooth Highway route ending in the Badlands.

airports.name: Minot International Airport
airports.note: North-central ND. Pairs with Scandinavian Heritage Park and a River Road approach.

airports.name: Hector International Airport
airports.note: Largest airport in North Dakota with the most connections. A full day's drive west across the state — a good road-trip in itself.
